title: struthanthus lucarquensis
intro: Struthanthus lucarquensis, commonly known as the 'tree-dwelling mistletoe', is a parasitic plant species that grows on the branches of host trees, such as the Chilean guanacaste. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Struthanthus lucarquensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of Struthanthus lucarquensis often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically in the form of infusions or poultices.
